title: Browser Origins, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Fields · Contract Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective browser request trust and cross-origin policy; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in browser request trust and cross-origin policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, CORS read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization.
Validate exact origins, methods, requested headers, credential mode, exposed headers, max age, Vary, null origin, and rejection behavior.
Use safe-method invariants, Go CrossOriginProtection, Sec-Fetch-Site and Origin policy, tokens when needed, and narrowly reviewed bypasses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Inspect the failure record · Debug Origin Site Cors Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 7464 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to keep CORS response sharing separate from CSRF mutation authorization; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, CORS read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization.

Inspect the Origin Site Cors Boundary trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Go server Inspect the failure record · Debug Origin Site Cors Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, cors read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization. For Go server Inspect the failure record · Debug Origin Site Cors Boundary, keep Origin Site Cors Boundary connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using content and type plus specific case evidence.
